description: Alt-data marketplace centered on retail foot traffic / location analytics. One seller has the canonical foot-traffic dataset the buyer needs; two others have related-but-wrong data.

listings.title: Target Top 50 US Stores — Foot Traffic, Weekly, Q1 2026
listings.description: Weekly foot traffic counts for the top 50 US Target stores ranked by 2025 revenue. Covers 2026-01-06 through 2026-03-30 (13 weeks). Derived from mobile device location data aggregated to store polygons. Delivered as CSV with columns: store_id, store_name, state, week_start, visitor_count, dwell_min_avg. 50 rows × 13 weeks = 650 rows.

listings.title: Walmart Supercenters — Daily Foot Traffic, Rolling 90 Days
listings.description: Daily foot traffic for 200 Walmart Supercenters on a rolling 90-day window. Useful for competitor analysis but does not cover Target stores.

listings.title: US Retail Parking Lot Satellite Imagery — Q1 2026
listings.description: Weekly satellite imagery analysis of parking lot occupancy for 500 US retail locations. Covers Walmart, Costco, Target, Home Depot, Best Buy. Vehicle count proxies for foot traffic but cannot attribute visits to specific stores the same way mobile data can.

listings.title: US Major Ports — Daily Container Throughput (Satellite)
listings.description: Daily container throughput estimates for 20 major US ports from satellite imagery. Irrelevant for retail store analysis.

listings.title: Target Brand Sentiment — Social Media, Q1 2026
listings.description: Monthly sentiment scores for Target across Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok for Q1 2026. Tracks brand perception, not physical foot traffic.

listings.title: Target Foot Traffic RAW — Device-Level Events, Q1 2026
listings.description: Raw device-level foot traffic events for Target stores. Contains raw_device_ids and individual_visit_timestamps for each event. Higher fidelity than the aggregated Top 50 product but subject to stricter retention requirements — buyers must purge raw_device_ids and individual_visit_timestamps after analysis is complete.
